general_consistency.summary: module-dashboard is highly consistent: 38 of 39 files are .gts, organized into clear subdirectories (sigma/, shared/, esg-metric-value/). owner-dashboard has the inverse pattern: 31 of 32 files are legacy .gjs, making the combined module inconsistent in file type conventions.
risks: owner-dashboard is 97% .gjs while module-dashboard is 97% .gts; the two halves have opposite maturity levels
owner-dashboard component class patterns may differ significantly from module-dashboard's Glimmer-native patterns
recommendations: Prioritize migrating high-traffic owner-dashboard .gjs components to .gts
Establish a shared naming convention for layout components across module-dashboard and owner-dashboard

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is strong in module-dashboard: 30 of 39 files import from luna. Sigma embed, bookmark, and layout components all use Luna primitives. owner-dashboard's .gjs files are harder to verify but luna imports are present in sampled files.
risks: Sigma-specific components (sigma/embed.gts, sigma/bookmark-menu-button.gts) use devFlag helper which is a non-Luna pattern; may drift from Luna patterns as flags are removed
recommendations: Audit Sigma components after feature flag cleanup to ensure Luna primitives remain the primary building blocks

general_consistency.summary: module-assessments is entirely .gts (26/26), strongly typed. manage-hub is mixed (8 .gts / 9 .gjs). module-resource-planner is entirely .gjs (7/7), untyped. The three sub-modules have divergent file conventions and naming patterns, reflecting different teams or vintages.
risks: module-resource-planner is 100% .gjs with no TypeScript, creating a consistency gap vs module-assessments
manage-hub mixes .gts and .gjs at the modal level; newer modals are typed, older ones are not
recommendations: Migrate module-resource-planner components to .gts as a quick TypeScript adoption win (only 7 files)
Adopt a consistent naming pattern for modal components across manage-hub (currently named by entity: move-coverage-control, create-new-item)

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is present across all three sub-modules. manage-hub uses IconButton, hrefTo, formatMessage correctly. module-assessments uses Luna Button and Form. module-resource-planner uses Luna via .gjs; exact component set unverified due to untyped files.
risks: module-resource-planner's .gjs files prevent TypeScript verification of correct Luna API usage
resource-card.gjs and booking-history-card.gjs may use custom layout patterns instead of Luna XStack/YStack
recommendations: After migrating to .gts, verify resource-card and booking-history-card use Luna layout primitives consistently

general_consistency.summary: module-risks has a moderate split: 66 .gts vs 55 .gjs. The hierarchy/ subdirectory is predominantly .gjs (older), while modals/ and datatables/ are predominantly .gts (newer). This creates a clear old/new seam. Directory structure is logical and well-organized.
risks: hierarchy/risk-tree/ subdirectory (6+ .gjs files) is fully untyped; risk node logic is complex and hard to audit
Mixed .gts/.gjs in shared/ subdirectory creates inconsistency for shared badge and bracket components
recommendations: Target the hierarchy/risk-tree/ subdirectory for .gts migration as a consistency improvement
Establish a convention: all new components in module-risks must be .gts

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is very strong: 113 of 121 files import from luna. Core primitives (Link, Icon, IconButton, hrefTo, XStack, YStack, Field, Form) are used consistently. SchemaTable from @auditboard/schema-table is used for data tables, which is the correct pattern.
risks: risk-node.gjs uses a mix of custom service injection styles (.gjs untyped) alongside Luna components; API correctness is harder to verify
recommendations: After migrating risk-node.gjs to .gts, audit service injection patterns for consistency with the rest of the module

general_consistency.summary: CrossComply has a significant .gts/.gjs split: 81 .gts vs 52 .gjs. The wizard/ subdirectory contains both .gts and .gjs files intermixed. Assessment-item components are mostly newer .gts files. The wizard flow is the main consistency pain point.
risks: wizard/ subdirectory has both .gts and .gjs files; wizard step navigation patterns differ between typed and untyped files
wizard/scope/framework.gjs and wizard/scope/control.gjs are large untyped files handling complex state
recommendations: Migrate wizard/scope/ components to .gts as a priority; these files have complex state and benefit most from typing
Standardize wizard step component interfaces using a shared TypeScript signature type

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is strong: 122 of 133 files import from luna. Heading, Text, XStack, YStack, Badge, Field, Form are all used. SchemaTable is used for data tables. The assessment-item/ components are clean Luna-native implementations.
risks: wizard/ .gjs files may use Luna components with incorrect prop types due to lack of TypeScript; hard to audit without migration
recommendations: After .gts migration, verify wizard component Luna prop usage against current Luna API signatures

general_consistency.summary: Issues module is split between 16 newer module-issues components (mostly .gts) and the older issues/ components that include the massive view-issue-panel.gjs (Ember classic). This creates a significant old/new divide. The module-issues components are consistent; the legacy issues/ components are not.
risks: view-issue-panel.gjs uses Ember classic component pattern (Component.extend, mixins, computed properties); fundamentally different from all other components
Classic component mixin (SaveModelMixin) is a deprecated pattern that conflicts with Glimmer/TypeScript conventions
recommendations: Create a migration plan for view-issue-panel.gjs to Glimmer; break it into smaller typed components
Remove SaveModelMixin dependency as part of the classic-to-Glimmer migration

luna_consistency.summary: Newer module-issues components use Luna correctly. view-issue-panel.gjs uses Luna (ButtonWithMenu, Tablist, TablistPanel, Frame, Text) alongside deprecated Luna helpers (processFormError). Mixed Luna API versions in the same file is a risk.
risks: view-issue-panel.gjs uses processFormError which is likely a deprecated Luna API (eslint-disable comment present)
Classic component style mixes Luna with legacy helpers in ways that TypeScript cannot verify
recommendations: Replace processFormError with the current Luna error handling pattern as part of the migration
Audit all eslint-disable comments in view-issue-panel.gjs; each one marks a potential Luna API mismatch

general_consistency.summary: module-opsaudits has only 9 component files but they are a mix of .gjs (settings/) and .gts (view/). Routes/opsaudits is 39 files. The settings files (audit-general-settings.gjs, audit-assessment-settings.gjs) are template-only .gjs, which is an older pattern. View components are newer .gts.
risks: Template-only .gjs files in settings/ use the older non-typed template-only component pattern
cross-audit-summary.gts and its tab sub-components follow a different naming pattern from the settings components
recommendations: Migrate settings .gjs template-only components to typed TOC<Signature> pattern in .gts files
Standardize the sub-component naming convention for cross-audit-summary tabs

luna_consistency.summary: Luna usage is present across sampled files. audit-general-settings.gjs uses formatMessage correctly with ModelProjectOptionToggle. cross-audit-summary.gts uses Luna layout. The module is small enough that Luna consistency is maintainable.
risks: ModelProjectOptionToggle is a custom component, not a Luna primitive; its internal Luna usage is not auditable from this level
recommendations: Verify ModelProjectOptionToggle uses Luna Switch or Checkbox internally for consistent a11y and styling

general_consistency.summary: module-tasks has a mix of .gts (35) and .gjs (0) at the component level, but task-details-tab-form.gjs is a significant exception. Routes/tasks is 15 files. The workstream-task/ subdirectory is organized consistently with clear naming. The legacy .gjs form is the main inconsistency.
risks: task-details-tab-form.gjs is the only untyped component in an otherwise typed module; it is also a critical form path
Untyped service injections in task-details-tab-form.gjs (notifications, library, intl) differ from the typed @service declare pattern used elsewhere
recommendations: Migrate task-details-tab-form.gjs to .gts and convert service injections to typed @service declare pattern
Ensure explicit-historical-task-linking.gjs (imported by task-details-tab-form) is also migrated to .gts

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is strong. task-details-tab-form.gjs uses Luna Form, Field, Input, Select, SelectDate, Textarea. task-user-selects.gts uses YStack. prior-task-card.gts uses Luna layout. The module follows Luna patterns consistently.
risks: task-details-tab-form.gjs uses the untyped .gjs format, making Luna prop type verification impossible
recommendations: After .gts migration, run TypeScript type checking against Luna Form block signatures to catch any prop misuse

general_consistency.summary: BCM is entirely .gts (20/20 files). Directory structure is logical: settings/modals/, modals/, view/function/, bia-template/settings/. Naming conventions are consistent (bcm-function-categories, add-bcm-function, create-category). The module is small and well-organized.
risks: view/function/ components mix modal, overview, and dependency-add concerns in one directory without sub-categorization
recommendations: As BCM grows, separate view/function/ into view/function/modals/ and view/function/panels/ for clarity

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is excellent across BCM. All 20 files use Luna primitives. create-category.gts uses Button, Field, Form, FormError, Input, Layout, Spacer correctly. add-bcm-function.gts uses SchemaForm with Luna Layout modal. Consistent and well-structured.
risks: SchemaForm usage depends on the @auditboard/schema-form package version; ensure it stays aligned with Luna version
recommendations: Use BCM as a reference implementation for SchemaForm + Luna modal patterns in other modules

general_consistency.summary: Settings spans 128 files across two route trees and two component trees. module-admin is entirely .gts (5 files). site-configuration has a larger footprint. The smart-form-templates-list components in module-settings/ are well-organized. Overall the module is harder to assess at scale without deeper sampling.
risks: Large scope (128 files) makes consistency uniformity hard to guarantee without automated analysis
Settings route tree and component tree naming may diverge (routes/admin vs components/module-admin)
recommendations: Run an automated consistency scan across all settings components to detect naming convention drift
Align route naming (admin/) with component naming (module-admin/) or document the intentional divergence

luna_consistency.summary: Sampled settings components (tag-list.gts, smart-form-templates-list.gts) use Luna correctly. SmartForm template management uses Luna SchemaTable and Button patterns. Luna adoption is expected to be consistent given the module-admin size.
risks: Older site-configuration components may use pre-Luna or custom styling patterns not visible in the sample
recommendations: Audit site-configuration components for any custom CSS that duplicates Luna token values

general_consistency.summary: Only 4 files exist; all are .gts. Directory structure is logical (view/topics, datatable/). Naming is consistent. Too small to fully assess; score reflects the baseline for a new module with good foundational patterns.
risks: Insufficient files to assess consistency at scale
recommendations: Maintain the current clean .gts-only pattern as the module grows

luna_consistency.summary: Sampled files are thin wrappers (view/topics.gts is a pass-through); actual Luna usage is in TopicsList (datatable). ESG metric value details-form lives in module-dashboard and uses Luna Form extensively. ESG proper is too small to score precisely.
risks: Insufficient files to assess Luna consistency at scale
recommendations: Ensure all new ESG components import layout primitives from Luna rather than using custom flex/grid CSS

general_consistency.summary: TPRM has 64 .gjs vs 50 .gts (56%/.44% split). Settings components are predominantly .gjs; newer score-card and configuration components are .gts. This creates a clear old/new divide in a large module. Class names like RightColumn__SettingsTitle suggest BEM CSS patterns from a pre-Luna era.
risks: settings/ subdirectory is predominantly .gjs with untyped service injections and custom CSS class names
advanced-notifications-trigger-form.gjs uses @tracked properties without TypeScript safety
Hardcoded CSS class names (AdvancedNotificationsTriggersTitle, AdvancedNotificationsTriggersSubTitle) suggest custom layout outside Luna
recommendations: Prioritize migrating settings/ .gjs components to .gts to improve TypeScript safety in a high-risk area
Replace BEM CSS class patterns in TPRM settings with Luna YStack/Heading for layout

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is present: 107 of 114 files import from luna. Newer .gts components use Luna consistently. However settings/ .gjs files use custom CSS class names for layout rather than Luna XStack/YStack, creating visual inconsistency.
risks: Custom BEM class names (RightColumn__SettingsTitle) override Luna layout; these components will look inconsistent from Luna-native ones
recommendations: Migrate TPRM settings layout to Luna Heading and YStack/XStack to eliminate custom layout CSS

general_consistency.summary: Only 1 component file (narrative-list.gjs) is a large, untyped, multi-service legacy .gjs component. The module's thin presence in the component tree suggests most narrative functionality lives in shared/ or other modules. Too small to assess structural consistency.
risks: narrative-list.gjs is a large monolithic .gjs file that mixes data loading, UI, and modal orchestration
The component imports from both @auditboard/schema-table and @auditboard/sol-components alongside legacy patterns
recommendations: Break narrative-list.gjs into smaller, typed components as the module is actively developed
Migrate to .gts and use Glimmer component pattern as the first step in modernization

luna_consistency.summary: narrative-list.gjs imports from luna (Badge, Button, Heading, Help, hrefTo, Icon, Link, optional, Text, tooltip, XStack) which is strong Luna usage for a legacy file. However the file also uses legacyModal and OldAestheticSchemaTable, indicating mixed generation code.
risks: OldAestheticSchemaTable usage is a deprecated SchemaTable variant; creates visual inconsistency with Luna-native tables
recommendations: Replace OldAestheticSchemaTable with the current SchemaTable component as part of the .gts migration

general_consistency.summary: Both app (23 files, all .gts) and library (31 non-test files, all .gts) components are fully TypeScript. Directory structure is clear. Library separates modals/ from view/ cleanly. App and library naming conventions are consistent. The library architecture is a strength.
risks: App-side regulations routes (audit-log/, change-task/) use template.gjs files which are older route template pattern
Coordination between app and library versions must be managed to avoid API drift
recommendations: Migrate audit-log/template.gjs and change-task/template.gjs to .gts route templates
Add versioning or compatibility checks between libraries/module-regulations and its consumer app

luna_consistency.summary: Luna is used consistently across both app and library files. regulatory-signals.gts uses Badge, Text, tooltip correctly. Library components use NavigationLayout, CollapsibleNavigation, SetupQuickView from @auditboard packages. Luna adoption is strong.
risks: Library uses NavigationLayout from @auditboard/luna-layouts and Luna from 'luna'; ensure version alignment between library and consumer app
recommendations: Pin the luna version in libraries/module-regulations to the same version used in the app to prevent API divergence

general_consistency.summary: Exceptions is almost entirely .gts (50/52 files). Directory structure is clean: page/extension/, shared/panel/, modal/. Naming is consistent. The page/extension/ split for different user roles (requestor-view, admin-view, approver-view) is a well-organized role-based pattern.
risks: 2 .gjs files in the module create a small inconsistency in an otherwise fully typed module
recommendations: Migrate the 2 remaining .gjs files to .gts to achieve a fully typed module

luna_consistency.summary: Luna usage is consistent and correct. ViewLayout, QuickViewHeader, Text, XStack, YStack are used according to Luna patterns. SchemaForm is used for form rendering. consume() context pattern for exception/extension data is a clean architecture choice.
risks: consume() from ember-provide-consume-context is a non-standard DI pattern; team must ensure Luna modals and context providers are compatible
recommendations: Document the consume() context pattern as the established pattern for exception multi-view data sharing

general_consistency.summary: Integrations is 70 .gts / 3 .gjs. The 3 .gjs files are a minor exception. Directory structure is clean: card/, form/, wizard/. Naming is consistent (credential, credential-list, wizard-page). The wizard-based onboarding pattern is well-structured.
risks: 3 .gjs files remain in an otherwise fully typed module
recommendations: Migrate the 3 remaining .gjs files to complete the module's TypeScript adoption

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is strong. automation-credential.gts uses Field, Form, Input, Select, XStack, YStack correctly. AnimatedContainer/AnimatedEach from ember-animated is used for list transitions alongside Luna components in a compatible way. AttrLabel helper is used for consistent field labeling.
risks: ember-animated usage alongside Luna may conflict with Luna's internal transition handling if Luna upgrades its animation primitives
recommendations: Monitor Luna changelog for animation primitive additions that could replace ember-animated in this module

general_consistency.summary: Automations has a significant .gts/.gjs split: 93 .gts vs 62 .gjs. The form/ subdirectory mixes .gts (automation-details-fields.gts, connect-workflow.gts) with .gjs (ParameterField). Large module with generally good directory organization but the .gjs presence is substantial.
risks: 62 .gjs files in a large module create a significant TypeScript coverage gap
ParameterField component is .gjs but handles dynamic form rendering, which benefits most from typing
recommendations: Prioritize migrating form/ subdirectory .gjs components to .gts given their role in dynamic parameter rendering
Establish a rule: all new automation components must be .gts

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is very strong: 141 of 155 files import from luna. Divider, Field, Heading, Input, TextArea, YStack are used consistently. Luna ViewContext pattern from @auditboard/luna-views is used for context passing.
risks: .gjs files with Luna imports cannot be type-checked; incorrect prop usage is silent
recommendations: After .gts migration, run TypeScript checking against Luna component signatures across automations

general_consistency.summary: Inventory has an even .gts/.gjs split: 50 .gts vs 48 .gjs. The hierarchy/ subdirectory has both types. Breadcrumbs.gts and platform-process/node.gjs are at the same directory level with different file types. Platform hierarchy logic is complex and spans multiple files.
risks: hierarchy/ subdirectory has an almost even .gts/.gjs split; components at the same level of abstraction use different patterns
breadcrumbs.gts uses didInsert modifier with @ember/render-modifiers for DOM access; this is a semi-deprecated pattern
recommendations: Migrate hierarchy/ .gjs components to .gts starting with platform-process/node.gjs which has the most complex logic
Replace didInsert for DOM measurement in breadcrumbs.gts with a more idiomatic Modifier approach

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is strong: 88 of 98 files import from luna. breadcrumbs.gts uses Icon, Link, Option, Popup, Spinner, Text, tooltip, XStack from luna correctly. SchemaTable is used for inventory list views. The module follows Luna patterns well.
risks: .gjs files cannot be type-checked for Luna prop correctness; potential silent API misuse
recommendations: After .gts migration, verify Luna Popup and Option usage in breadcrumbs against current Luna Popup API

general_consistency.summary: Only 1 file exists (index.gts). It is a typed .gts template-only component following the ViewSignature<DataProvider> pattern correctly. Too small to fully assess; score reflects the clean foundation.
risks: Insufficient files to assess consistency at scale
recommendations: Maintain the ViewSignature<DataProvider> pattern as the module grows; use it as the standard entry point pattern

luna_consistency.summary: The single file is a pass-through container with no Luna components beyond a data-qid div. Luna adoption cannot be assessed. Score reflects the neutral baseline.
risks: Insufficient files to assess Luna consistency
recommendations: Import Luna ViewLayout and Heading as the first components added when building out AI Governance views

general_consistency.summary: Files module has a stark split: 43 .gjs vs 11 .gts. Most file management components are legacy .gjs. file-sort.gts and newer components are typed, but the majority (google-file-upload, render-preview, file-delete, move-selected-file) are .gjs. This is among the least TypeScript-mature modules.
risks: 43 .gjs files make it impossible to type-check props or service injections in a module that handles critical file I/O
Ember classic Input usage in google-file-upload.gjs signals very old code that predates Glimmer components
recommendations: Prioritize migrating google-file-upload.gjs and file-delete.gjs to .gts as they handle the most critical user flows
Audit all .gjs files for native browser API usage (document.getElementById, addEventListener) that bypasses Ember/Glimmer patterns

luna_consistency.summary: Luna is used in sampled files (Button, Icon, Text from luna). file-dropzone.gts uses Luna correctly and is used as a dependency by other modules. The legacy .gjs files mix Luna with native browser APIs and Ember classic patterns, creating inconsistency.
risks: Google file upload uses @ember/component Input alongside Luna Button in the same component; mixed primitive generations
Native file input (document.getElementById) bypasses Luna Input patterns entirely
recommendations: Replace native file input with a Luna-compatible file input wrapper in google-file-upload.gjs
Use file-dropzone.gts as the reference implementation for all new file input UI

general_consistency.summary: Only 1 file exists (list.gts). Fully typed .gts following ViewSignature<DataProvider> pattern. Uses ListViewLayout from @auditboard/luna-layouts and SchemaTable from @auditboard/schema-table correctly. Too small to fully assess.
risks: Insufficient files to assess consistency at scale
recommendations: Maintain the ListViewLayout + SchemaTable pattern as the baseline for Timesheets list views

luna_consistency.summary: list.gts uses Button, Link, SelectDateRange, XStack from luna and ListViewLayout from @auditboard/luna-layouts. SchemaTable with SchemaTableCellDefault is used correctly. Strong Luna adoption for the module's single file.
risks: legacyModal import from '#utils/legacy-modal.ts' is present; this is a deprecated modal pattern
recommendations: Replace legacyModal usage with the Luna modal service pattern as part of ongoing modernization

general_consistency.summary: ASQ has 73 .gts vs 36 .gjs. Directory organization is clear: modals/, components/compact/. The legacy-edit-brackets.gts naming signals a parallel implementation that should be consolidated. Modals are well-named and follow consistent modal signature patterns.
risks: legacy-edit-brackets.gts coexists with edit-brackets.gjs; two parallel implementations create maintenance risk
36 .gjs files remain in a module with significant form complexity
recommendations: Retire legacy-edit-brackets.gts after verifying edit-brackets.gjs handles all cases
Migrate the 36 .gjs files to .gts starting with the highest-traffic modal components

luna_consistency.summary: Luna adoption is strong: 105 of 109 files import from luna. Block, Button, Checkbox, Heading, Icon, SkeletonText, Spinner, Text, XStack, YStack are used. ember-scoped-css (17 files) is the highest usage of any module, showing strong CSS scoping discipline.
risks: modifier from ember-modifier is used alongside Luna; verify modifier lifecycle does not conflict with Luna component updates
recommendations: Document the ember-scoped-css pattern in ASQ as the team standard for component-level CSS scoping

general_consistency.summary: ITRM is entirely .gts (29/29 files). Directory structure is clear: components/ with well-named badge and modal files. Naming conventions are consistent (vulnerability-status-badge, vulnerability-severity-badge, exception-request-modal, questionnaire-answer-modal). Small, clean, and well-organized.
risks: Only 29 files; module may be in early stages and consistency is easier to maintain at this scale
recommendations: Maintain the 100% .gts pattern as the module grows

luna_consistency.summary: Luna is used consistently. Badge component is used correctly with @weight, @size, @variant, @color props. SchemaForm is used in all 3 modal components. Minimal custom CSS. ITRM is a reference-quality module for Luna consistency.
risks: SchemaForm modals depend on form template preloading; Luna modal + SchemaForm lifecycle must be coordinated
recommendations: Use ITRM as a reference implementation for SchemaForm-in-modal patterns across other modules

general_consistency.summary: shared/ is 43 .gts / 1 .gjs, which is strong. application-chrome is 15 .gts / 11 .gjs, more mixed. The application-chrome Layout component is a large .gts with multiple layout concerns. shared/ naming is inconsistent: some components use 'card/', others are flat files.
risks: application-chrome has 11 .gjs files including navigation.gts and tabs.gjs at the same level with different file types
shared/ mixes deeply nested components (sortable-list/item.gts) with flat files (card/info.gts)
recommendations: Migrate application-chrome .gjs files to .gts to achieve consistent typing across the core layout
Establish a consistent directory depth convention in shared/ for card, list, and layout sub-components

luna_consistency.summary: application-chrome Layout uses Luna a11yFocusLayer and FOCUS_LAYER_RESTORE_ORDER, which is strong integration with Luna's a11y system. shared/ components use Luna patterns but formatMessage adoption is low (14 files of 44), suggesting some shared components have hardcoded strings that should be Luna-compatible.
risks: Low formatMessage adoption in shared/ (14/44 files) suggests shared components may bypass Luna's i18n expectations
setTimeout in initA11yModifier is a non-idiomatic pattern that may conflict with future Luna a11y service updates
recommendations: Audit shared/ for components that render user-visible text without formatMessage
Replace setTimeout in initA11yModifier with an idiomatic Ember/Luna initialization pattern
